events.label: reasoning
events.text: We are asked to evaluate a scenario: "service disconnect pulled but no wait time/verification (SEQ)" in the context of high-voltage safety for an automotive powertrain service visit. The work is in progress. The condition is "installed-defective" possibly, but we are checking sequence. The issue is that the service disconnect was pulled, but there was no wait time or verification. This is a safety procedure violation. In high-voltage systems, after pulling the service disconnect, you must wait a specified period for capacitors to discharge and verify zero energy with a meter. Skipping this step creates a shock hazard. So the decision should be "fail". Risk is high/critical. Work stage is "in-progress". Component conditions: maybe "installed-defective" if the component was defective? But the description says "service disconnect pulled but no wait time/verification" - the component condition might be "installed-defective" only if the component itself is defective; here it's a procedural violation, not necessarily a defective component. The instruction says: "Component condition categories that may apply: installed-defective". But the list given: ["installed-defective", "non-compliant", "worn", "degraded", "failed"]. The scenario is about the procedure, not the component condition. So maybe no component conditions apply? But the instruction says "use an empty list if none apply". I'll leave it empty. Percent complete: pulling the disconnect might be partway, but without verification, work is incomplete. Estimate maybe 40%? I'll say 50% as a guess. Value: not asked. Sound source, event time, rate, order: not applicable. Workable: perhaps no further work until safety step is done. Findings: sequence violation. Actions: stop work, wait prescribed time, verify zero energy. Rationale: brief. References: OEM HV procedures, NFPA 70E. So output JSON accordingly.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": [],
  "percent_complete": 40,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Service disconnect pulled without mandatory wait time for capacitor discharge",
    "No verification of zero energy state performed before proceeding"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop all work immediately",
    "Enforce minimum wait time per OEM procedures",
    "Verify absence of voltage with a calibrated meter before any contact"
  ],
  "rationale": "Skipping the wait/verification step after pulling the service disconnect violates OEM and NFPA 70E safety sequences, creating an immediate shock hazard from stored energy. Work must not continue until safe conditions are confirmed.",
  "references": [
    "OEM HV procedures",
    "NFPA 70E-adjacent"
  ]
}
